complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Create Postgres Database Schema and Migrations for Atomic Swap System
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the database schema creation into logical components: 1) Design and create Users table with proper indexing and constraints, 2) Design and create Nonce Pool table with status management, 3) Design and create Swap Offers table with JSONB asset structures and GIN indexes, 4) Design and create Swap Transactions table with comprehensive tracking fields. Each subtask should include migration scripts, constraint validation, and performance optimization.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: This task involves designing a complex relational database schema with JSONB fields, multiple indexes, foreign key relationships, and custom constraints. The complexity comes from the need to handle both structured data (users, transactions) and semi-structured data (asset metadata), plus the requirement for performance optimization with proper indexing strategies. The JSONB validation and GIN indexes add technical complexity.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Implement NoncePoolManager Service for Durable Nonce Account Management
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Divide the NoncePoolManager into specialized components: 1) Implement pool initialization and configuration management, 2) Create thread-safe pool operations with mutex/semaphore locking, 3) Implement user assignment logic with database integration, 4) Create nonce value retrieval and caching system, 5) Implement nonce advancement with retry logic and error handling, 6) Create cleanup and maintenance operations with periodic jobs. Each subtask should include comprehensive error handling and monitoring.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: This is a highly complex task requiring thread-safe operations, blockchain integration, database coordination, and sophisticated error handling. The complexity stems from managing concurrent access to a shared resource pool, handling Solana RPC failures, implementing caching strategies, and ensuring data consistency across multiple systems. The thread-safety requirements and blockchain interaction patterns make this particularly challenging.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Implement FeeCalculator Service for Platform Fee Logic
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Structure the FeeCalculator into focused components: 1) Implement fee configuration management and validation with environment variable loading, 2) Create core fee calculation logic for different swap types (NFT-to-NFT, NFT-to-SOL, SOL-to-NFT) with percentage and flat fee support, 3) Implement fee breakdown generation, validation, and cap enforcement with detailed error handling.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: This task has moderate complexity due to the need to handle multiple fee calculation scenarios, percentage-based calculations, and fee cap enforcement. The business logic is straightforward but requires careful handling of edge cases, proper validation, and accurate mathematical calculations. The configuration management and error handling add some complexity but it's primarily a computational service.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Implement AssetValidator Service for NFT and cNFT Ownership Verification
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down asset validation into specialized verification components: 1) Implement standard NFT ownership verification with SPL token account queries and caching, 2) Create compressed NFT integration with indexer APIs and rate limiting, 3) Implement Merkle proof data management and validation, 4) Create asset normalization and unified response formatting, 5) Implement re-validation logic and edge case handling (burned, frozen, transferred assets).
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: This task is complex due to the need to handle two different NFT standards (standard and compressed), integrate with external APIs, implement Merkle proof validation, and handle various edge cases. The complexity comes from the dual validation paths, external API dependencies, caching strategies, and the need to handle blockchain state changes. The Merkle proof validation and indexer integration add significant technical complexity.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Implement TransactionBuilder Service for Constructing Atomic Swap Transactions
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Divide transaction building into specialized construction components: 1) Implement transaction foundation setup with durable nonce integration and size monitoring, 2) Create ATA management system for token account creation and optimization, 3) Implement standard NFT transfer instruction building, 4) Create compressed NFT transfer integration with Bubblegum program, 5) Implement SOL transfer and platform fee collection instruction building, 6) Create transaction signing, size optimization, and validation systems.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: This is one of the most complex tasks, requiring deep knowledge of Solana transaction construction, multiple program integrations (SPL Token, Bubblegum, System Program), transaction size optimization, and proper instruction ordering. The complexity stems from handling multiple asset types, ensuring atomic execution, managing transaction size limits, integrating with multiple blockchain programs, and implementing sophisticated error handling. The durable nonce integration and size optimization add significant technical challenges.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Implement OfferManager Service for Core Swap Business Logic
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Structure the OfferManager into comprehensive business logic components: 1) Implement offer creation and validation with asset verification, 2) Create counter-offer system with chain management and validation, 3) Implement offer cancellation with nonce invalidation, 4) Create offer acceptance workflow with transaction building coordination, 5) Implement post-transaction management and state updates, 6) Create database transaction management with proper locking and rollback, 7) Implement service coordination and error handling across all dependencies.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: This task represents the core business logic orchestrator with high complexity due to the need to coordinate multiple services, manage complex state transitions, handle database transactions, and implement sophisticated error handling. The complexity comes from managing the complete offer lifecycle, coordinating with all other services, handling concurrent operations, and ensuring data consistency across multiple systems. The service coordination and transaction management aspects make this particularly challenging.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Completely Rewrite Solana Escrow Program for Atomic Swaps
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Structure the Solana program rewrite into specialized components: 1) Design new program architecture and account structures, 2) Implement atomic swap instruction with basic asset transfer logic, 3) Integrate compressed NFT support with Bubblegum program, 4) Implement platform fee collection system with treasury PDA, 5) Create on-chain fee accounting and withdrawal mechanisms, 6) Implement comprehensive security and validation framework, 7) Create program testing suite with unit and integration tests, 8) Generate IDL and TypeScript client SDK.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: This is the most complex task requiring expert-level Solana program development, including program architecture design, multiple program integrations, PDA management, security implementation, and comprehensive testing. The complexity stems from the need to completely rewrite existing functionality, integrate with multiple Solana programs (Bubblegum, SPL Token, System Program), implement sophisticated fee collection, ensure security against various attack vectors, and create a complete testing and client generation suite.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Implement Express HTTP API Routes for Atomic Swaps
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Structure the API implementation into focused route groups: 1) Set up Express middleware chain with authentication, validation, and error handling, 2) Implement offer management routes (create, counter-offer, cancel), 3) Implement transaction execution routes (accept, confirm), 4) Create query routes with filtering and pagination, 5) Implement comprehensive error handling, input validation, and security measures.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: This task has moderate-to-high complexity due to the need to implement multiple API endpoints with proper authentication, validation, error handling, and integration with the OfferManager service. The complexity comes from implementing secure authentication, comprehensive input validation, proper HTTP status code handling, and coordinating with the complex business logic layer. The API design and security considerations add significant complexity.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Implement Monitoring and Background Jobs System
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Organize monitoring and background jobs into specialized systems: 1) Implement comprehensive health check system with component monitoring, 2) Create automated cleanup jobs for expired offers and unused nonce accounts, 3) Implement nonce pool replenishment job with threshold management, 4) Create structured logging system with correlation IDs and log management, 5) Implement error alerting system with notification integration and throttling.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: This task has moderate-to-high complexity due to the need to implement multiple background systems, job scheduling, health monitoring, and alerting mechanisms. The complexity comes from coordinating multiple background processes, implementing reliable job scheduling, creating comprehensive monitoring, and integrating with external notification systems. The need for proper error handling and system observability adds significant complexity.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Clean Up and Migrate Old Agreement Code
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: This task involves systematic code cleanup and preservation without breaking existing functionality. Focus on commenting out old agreement-related code while preserving it for future reference, ensuring receipts functionality remains operational, and creating comprehensive migration documentation.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: This is a relatively straightforward task involving code cleanup and documentation. The complexity is low because it primarily involves commenting out existing code, updating documentation, and ensuring no breaking changes. The main challenges are ensuring thorough identification of all agreement-related code and maintaining system functionality during the cleanup process.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Structure configuration management into organized components: 1) Define comprehensive environment variable schema with validation, 2) Implement configuration loading and validation system with type safety, 3) Update constants and create configuration accessor functions, 4) Implement configuration caching, refresh mechanisms, and developer experience improvements.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: This task has moderate complexity due to the need to implement type-safe configuration management, comprehensive validation, and developer-friendly interfaces. The complexity comes from ensuring proper validation of all configuration parameters, implementing type safety, creating accessor patterns, and handling configuration changes. The need for security (sensitive data handling) and developer experience adds moderate complexity.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Create Comprehensive Test Suite for Atomic Swap System
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Organize the test suite into comprehensive testing layers: 1) Create unit tests for all core services (NoncePoolManager, FeeCalculator, AssetValidator, TransactionBuilder), 2) Implement integration tests for OfferManager with service coordination, 3) Create end-to-end swap flow tests for all asset combinations, 4) Implement API endpoint testing with authentication and validation, 5) Create specialized test scenarios for edge cases and error recovery, 6) Set up test infrastructure with database, blockchain integration, and CI/CD.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: This task has high complexity due to the need to create comprehensive tests across multiple layers (unit, integration, end-to-end), mock complex dependencies, set up test infrastructure, and achieve high code coverage. The complexity stems from testing blockchain interactions, mocking external services, creating realistic test scenarios, and ensuring tests are reliable and maintainable. The need for comprehensive coverage across all system components adds significant complexity.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Update Documentation for Atomic Swap Architecture
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Structure documentation into comprehensive sections: 1) Create API and database documentation with examples and schemas, 2) Document architecture components, security considerations, and system flows, 3) Create deployment guides, migration instructions, and operational documentation.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: This task has moderate complexity due to the comprehensive scope of documentation required and the need to ensure accuracy across all system components. The complexity comes from documenting complex technical systems, creating accurate examples, maintaining consistency across different documentation types, and ensuring the documentation remains useful for different audiences (developers, operators, users). The dependency on multiple other tasks adds coordination complexity.
